lessons.title: My Mother and Father
lessons.topic: Core family and sounds of U and V

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Who drives a car in the family?
Who reads a bedtime story?
questions.hint: She tucks you in at night.

questions.question_text: Who has glasses in the family?

steps.title: Family Day
steps.text: This is my mother. She has a yellow umbrella. My father drives a big van. At home, we laugh together.

questions.question: What color is the mother's umbrella?

steps.explanation: Use 'I have a' to show something you own.

steps.audio_text: Hi! I see my van. My father is driving. He has a blue umbrella.

questions.question_text: This is my ___. (Grammar question)
Which word sounds like 'van'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'Father' is a family member.
What do you use when it rains? (Reading comprehension)
Which sound does 'umbrella' start with?

lessons.title: Brother and Sister
lessons.topic: Siblings and sounds of W and X

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Who says 'I love my family!' in the video?
What sound does the letter W make in the word 'wonderful'?
In the video, who plays with a toy car?
questions.hint: Look for the boy with the toy.

steps.title: Our Family Day
steps.text: This is my brother. My brother has a big box. My sister is here. She is looking at her watch. We are playing together!

questions.question: Who is looking at a watch?

steps.explanation: Use this to show your family.

steps.audio_text: I have a brother and a sister. My brother likes to wear a watch. We keep his watch in a blue box.

questions.question: How many siblings do I have?

questions.question_text: What is the sound of W in 'watch'?
What is inside the box? (Reading comprehension question)
I have a ___ . (Grammar question)
True or False: A sister is a boy.
Which word means 'girl sibling'? (Vocabulary question)

lessons.title: Grandpa and Baby
lessons.topic: Extended family and sounds of Y and Z

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What animal sound does Grandma make when she laughs? 🐢
questions.hint: It's a slow and steady sound.

questions.question_text: What does Baby say when it wants to sleep? 😴
questions.hint: Think of the sound people make when they sleep.

questions.question_text: What does Grandpa wear when he goes outside? 🧢
questions.hint: It goes on your head to keep you warm.

steps.title: Family Fun Day
steps.text: This is my grandpa. He plays with a yo-yo. My grandma gives me a zebra toy. The baby laughs. We all have fun!

steps.explanation: Use this to talk about family.

steps.audio_text: My grandpa is funny. He plays with a yo-yo. My grandma and I watch the baby giggle.

questions.question_text: What sound does 'yo-yo' start with? (Vocabulary question)
My grandpa is ___. (Grammar question)
Who watches the baby giggle in the story? (Reading comprehension question)
True or False: Grandpa has a zebra. (Comprehension question)
Does 'zebra' start with a 'Z' sound? (Vocabulary question)

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What does the animal with a long neck eat?
questions.hint: The animal uses its long neck to reach high in trees.

questions.question_text: Which animal in the video is known as the king of the jungle?
Which animal has a trunk?
questions.hint: The animal uses its trunk to drink water.

steps.title: Lily's Family Day
steps.text: Hello! I am Lily. This is my mother and father. I have a big brother. My grandma is here. We have a blue car. I am excited!

steps.audio_text: Welcome to my garden. I have a yellow hat and a big green apple. My sister is playing.

steps.expected_text: this is my mother and this is my father

questions.question_text: This is my ___ brother. (Grammar question)
Which word is a family member? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'My sister is a teacher.' means my sister teaches.
What is 'My father is tall.' about? (Reading comprehension question)
I have a ___ car. (Grammar question)
